content.title: Microsoft introduces TypeScript 5.8: Explore the latest features and enhancements - Neowin
TypeScript 5.8 improves interoperability between CommonJS and ECMAScript | heise online
TypeScript 5.8 Released with Improved Inference and ...

content.text:
## File Search Results

I found **2 TypeScript files** in the `src/` directory:
- `src/index.ts`
- `src/utils.ts`

## TypeScript 5.8 Release Information
TypeScript 5.8 has been officially released

, bringing several significant improvements and new features:

### Key Features

**Granular Checks for Return Expressions**:
TypeScript 5.8 added more granular checks for branches within return expressions
The new version checks each branch of the conditional expression against the declared return type of the contained functions, if present

.

**Improved Module Interoperability**:
The new version brings improved interoperability of CommonJS modules with the newer ECMAScript modules, which should simplify the work of library developers
Previously failing CommonJS require statements now work seamlessly under --module nodenext

.

**Direct TypeScript Execution**:
Since Node.js 23.6 and with the new --erasableSyntaxOnly flag, TypeScript can now run directly in Node.js without transpilation, as long as the syntax is erasable
The new compiler option "--erasableSyntaxOnly" has been added in 5.8, which prohibits TypeScript-only features such as enums, namespaces, parameter properties, and special import forms

.

### Performance Improvements

TypeScript 5.8 introduces a number of optimizations that can both improve the time to build up a program, and also to update a program based on a file change in either --watch mode or editor scenarios
TypeScript now avoids array allocations that would be involved while normalizing paths and operates more directly on indexes of the original path

.

### Installation
To get started using TypeScript 5.8, you can install it through npm
with the standard command, or
you can get it from NuGet or if you prefer npm

.

### Notable Changes from Beta
Since the beta release, some work on how functions with conditional return types are checked was pulled back and will be shipped in TypeScript 5.9
, though the granular return expression checks mentioned above were retained.
Thanks! Can you summarize what you found?
